title: U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation
authors: Olaf Ronneberger, Philipp Fischer, Thomas Brox
hook: U-Net lets computers draw precise outlines in images, even with few labels.
promise: You will understand how U-Net segments images so accurately with a unique architecture and minimal data.
prerequisite_note: Bring curiosity about how computers can find and outline objects within complex images.

glossary.plain: Dividing an image into regions, each representing a different object or structure.
A shortcut that passes information from earlier network layers to later ones, helping preserve details.
Part of the network that reduces image size to capture big-picture context.
Part of the network that increases image size to recover details and make pixel-level predictions.
Creating new training images by slightly altering existing ones, boosting limited datasets.
Training all steps of a model together, from input to output, in one unified process.

cards.title: How U-Net draws boundaries
body: You will understand how U-Net segments images so accurately with a unique architecture and minimal data.

cards.title: How can machines find shapes in images?
body: Imagine needing a computer to outline every cell in a microscope photo, but you only have a few labeled examples to teach it.
Before U-Net, even powerful neural networks required thousands of examples and still struggled with precise outlines. Biomedical imaging needed something better.

cards.title: The U-shaped journey from image to outline
body: U-Net processes images in two stages: it first compresses the picture to understand context, then expands it back while recovering fine details.
This U-shaped architecture lets U-Net combine big-picture context with sharp, local information, producing detailed segmentations.
cards.caption: U-Net shrinks and then expands images, using skip connections to preserve fine details.

cards.title: How does U-Net beat the sliding window?
body: Toggle between the classic sliding-window approach and U-Net's end-to-end architecture for segmenting a cell.
cards.offNote: Misses context, slow, and struggles with precise boundaries.
cards.onNote: Processes the whole image at once, uses context, and outlines shapes cleanly.

cards.title: U-Net's central trick: skip connections
body: U-Net copies features from the shrinking side and fuses them into the expanding side, letting the network recover lost details.
These skip connections act like bridges, carrying fine-grained information directly to where it is needed for sharp outlines.

cards.title: How much data does U-Net need?
body: Slide to see how U-Net performs with different amounts of labeled training data.
cards.low: U-Net still finds outlines, but some details are missed.
cards.mid: With moderate labels, U-Net draws most boundaries accurately.
cards.high: With plenty of labels, segmentations are crisp and nearly perfect.

cards.title: What makes U-Net work?
body: Pick the ingredients that make U-Net excel at segmentation with limited data.

choices.label: Skip connections
choices.note: Fuse fine details from early layers into later predictions.

choices.label: Symmetric architecture
choices.note: Matches shrinking and expanding paths for balanced processing.

choices.label: Data augmentation
choices.note: Generates more examples from few labeled images.

choices.label: End-to-end training
choices.note: Optimizes all steps together for best performance.

choices.label: Pixel-wise prediction
choices.note: Makes a label decision for every pixel in the image.

choices.label: Convolutional layers
choices.note: Extracts features at multiple scales for context and detail.

cards.title: U-Net's impact on biomedical imaging
body: U-Net set a new standard for image segmentation, especially in medical and scientific images where labels are scarce.
It not only won major competitions but also made accurate segmentation practical in real labs and clinics.
cards.caption: U-Net achieved high accuracy and efficiency, transforming biomedical image analysis.

cards.title: Why U-Net is a classic
body: U-Net showed that clever architecture can make deep learning work even when data is scarce, a common scenario in science and medicine.
Its design inspired countless later models and remains a foundation for benchmark-leading segmentation tasks today.

recap.label: Compress
recap.note: Shrink the image to capture context.

recap.label: Bridge
recap.note: Use skip connections to save details.

recap.label: Expand
recap.note: Grow the image back for fine predictions.
